title: Dim an LED with a Potentiometer
description: Adjust LED brightness using a potentiometer and an Arduino.

dialogue.text: Orion here again! Ready to add some interactivity? Let's use a potentiometer to control the brightness of your LED.

options.text: Sounds good! What do I need?

dialogue.text: Grab a potentiometer, connect the ends to 5V and ground, and the middle pin to analog pin A0. The LED stays on pin 9 with a 220 ohm resistor.
Use analogRead() to read the potentiometer and analogWrite() on pin 9 to adjust brightness. The Arduino IDE's 'AnalogInput' example shows the basics.

options.text: Uploaded! The LED changes brightness as I turn the knob.

dialogue.text: Great job! Potentiometers open up lots of control options. Try using them with motors or sensors next!
